LAW & REALITY

The Real Cost of Dowry

20+ Women die every day in India due to dowry-related violence (NCRB data).
5 Years Minimum imprisonment for demanding or accepting dowry.
1961 The year Dowry was outlawed via the Dowry Prohibition Act.

Dowry Prohibition Act, 1961

Under Section 3 and 4 of the Dowry Prohibition Act, giving, taking, or even demanding dowry in any form is a cognizable and non-bailable offense. Violators face a minimum of 5 years in prison and a fine of ₹15,000 or the value of the dowry, whichever is higher.

"Dowry is not a tradition. It is a transactional custom disguised as culture that reduces human beings to commodities and breeds systemic abuse."

How it manifests today:

While direct "cash demands" are sometimes avoided, it takes modern forms under phrases like "We want nothing, just organize a grand wedding", "Give whatever you want to your daughter", or demanding luxury cars, sponsorships for foreign education, or lavish apartment bookings.
